name: claude-skills
description: Ryan Krol's personal Claude Code plugins and skills.

plugins.name: implementation-harness
plugins.description: Scaffold a Ralph-style single-loop, CI-gated TASKS.json implementation harness into any project, author its backlog, and operate it — via skills. Three global skills (create/customize/upgrade) bootstrap and reconcile an install; six operational skills are scaffolded project-locally and kept in sync by upgrade, so their logic can never drift ahead of a given project's `.harness/` version. Data-driven difficulty auto-tuning (faceted escalation calibration), a portable dashboard (backlog, an ideas inbox with per-idea cards, per-facet harness internals, and a live loop-status strip), an ideas-to-tasks pipeline, operational skills for pre-run vetting, interrupt recovery, and reviewing failed tasks into follow-ups, and an upgrade skill that reconciles an existing install with newer plugin versions — including adopting legacy or hand-forked installs. A `custom/` overlay makes project customization upgrade-safe (conventions, lifecycle hooks, a secret-guard denylist, visual-verify / build-audit prompt injection, a dashboard title), surfaced through a guided customization walkthrough skill on create, on upgrade, or on demand. Distinct from the official ralph-loop plugin.

plugins.name: update-plugins
plugins.description: One command to update everything: refreshes every configured marketplace and updates every installed plugin to its latest version, via the `claude` CLI's own scriptable subcommands (not the interactive session slash commands, which have no bulk-update option). A single skill, nothing else.
